title: Kenneth Arnold Sighting
description: Private pilot Kenneth Arnold reports nine crescent-shaped objects near Mt. Rainier, WA. A journalist coins the term 'flying saucer'. The modern UFO era begins.

title: Roswell Incident
description: RAAF public affairs announces recovery of a 'flying disc' near Roswell, NM. The Army retracted the statement within 24 hours, claiming it was a weather balloon. Remains the most debated UAP case in history.

title: Washington D.C. Flap — Project Blue Book
description: UFOs appear on radar at Washington National Airport and Andrews AFB. Interceptors scrambled. Project Blue Book, the Air Force's official UAP investigation, ramps up in response.

title: Project Blue Book Closed
description: The USAF officially closes Project Blue Book after investigating 12,618 sightings. 701 remain 'unidentified'. The Condon Report recommends ending Air Force UFO investigations.

title: Belgian UFO Wave
description: Over 13,500 people including police and military personnel report large triangular craft across Belgium. The Belgian Air Force scrambles F-16s and confirms radar lock on unknown objects.

title: Phoenix Lights
description: Thousands of Arizona residents report a massive V-shaped craft up to a mile wide moving silently over Phoenix. Witnessed by Governor Fife Symington, who later admitted he saw it himself.

title: USS Nimitz Incident
description: Navy F/A-18F pilots intercept a 'Tic-Tac' shaped object off the California coast. The object demonstrated instantaneous acceleration, no visible propulsion, and defiance of known aerodynamics. Cmdr. David Fravor reported the encounter.

title: NYT / AATIP Disclosure
description: The New York Times reveals the Pentagon's secret Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP), run by Luis Elizondo. Declassified UAP videos (Nimitz, Gimbal, GoFast) published for first time.

title: Navy Officially Acknowledges UAP Videos
description: The US Navy officially confirms the three released UAP videos are authentic and depict unidentified aerial phenomena. A formal UAP reporting process is established for Navy pilots.

title: DNI UAP Preliminary Assessment
description: The Director of National Intelligence releases an unclassified UAP report to Congress. Of 144 reports, only one could be explained. The remaining 143 are 'unresolved' — a landmark shift in official posture.

title: AARO Established
description: The All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) is established within the Department of Defense as the central UAP investigation body, with a mandate to coordinate across military and intelligence agencies.

title: Congressional UAP Hearings
description: The House Oversight Committee holds the first major congressional UAP hearing since 1966. Witnesses including David Grusch, Ryan Graves, and Cmdr. David Fravor testify under oath about non-human intelligence and crash retrieval programs.

title: AARO Historical Record Volume 1
description: AARO releases its first Historical Record Report, examining over 70 years of UAP claims including alleged crash retrieval and reverse-engineering programs. Finds no verifiable evidence of extraterrestrial craft — but acknowledges significant classification barriers.
